proven.claim: Proven property: the length of any JavaScript array is ≥ 0.
proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P5
; Proven property: the length of any JavaScript array is ≥ 0.
; We model the two lengths that the `printSummary` line reads.
(declare-const files_len Int)        ; result.graph.files.length
(declare-const contracts_len Int)    ; result.derivation.contracts.length

; Transition: the code does NOT modify these values – they are given by the
; runtime as the length of a concrete array.  By definition of an array length
; we constrain them to be non‑negative.
(assert (>= files_len 0))
(assert (>= contracts_len 0))

; Negate the property we want to prove (that a length could be negative).
(assert (or (< files_len 0) (< contracts_len 0)))

(check-sat)
; Expected: unsat → a negative length is impossible, so the property is proven.
